disclaimer: Educational wellness information only — not a prescription, diagnosis, or instruction to start/stop medicines. Always confirm with your doctor or pharmacist.

uses: Often used for fever with cold when bacterial infection is suspected
medicines.how_to_take: Usually twice daily (1-0-1). Complete the full course unless doctor says otherwise.
avoid_if: Known penicillin/cephalosporin allergy — ask doctor first

alternatives.name: Cefixime (as prescribed)
alternatives.reason: Same antibiotic family — only if doctor switches

alternatives.name: Amoxicillin-Clavulanate (as prescribed)
alternatives.reason: Common alternative antibiotic class for respiratory infections

medicines.how_to_take: Often 1-1-1 or 1-0-1 after food. Do not exceed dose on the slip.
common_side_effects: Rare kidney strain with long use

alternatives.name: Paracetamol alone (as prescribed)
alternatives.reason: Gentler on stomach for fever/pain if NSAID not wanted

alternatives.name: Ibuprofen (as prescribed)
alternatives.reason: Another NSAID option — only with doctor advice

medicines.generic_name: Commonly an antihistamine / cold-relief brand (confirm exact salt on pack)
medicines.how_to_take: Often once at night (0-0-1) as on many slips — follow your prescription.
avoid_if: If you need to drive/operate machines and feel sleepy

medicines.how_to_take: Usually once or twice daily before food (B/F).
avoid_if: Discuss with doctor if on certain HIV/antifungal medicines

medicines.how_to_take: Commonly every 6–8 hours as needed; do not exceed daily max — ask pharmacist.
common_side_effects: Rare liver risk if overdose or with alcohol

alternatives.name: Ibuprofen (as prescribed)
alternatives.reason: If inflammation pain dominates and stomach is fine

medicines.how_to_take: Usually 2–3 times daily with food as prescribed.
Often once daily, preferably at night.
avoid_if: Caution if operating machinery when drowsy

medicines.how_to_take: With meals as prescribed — never change dose yourself.

alternatives.name: Discuss with your doctor only
alternatives.reason: Diabetes switches need lab review — no self-substitution
